rulings.Case Title: PRO UNLIMITED INC. VS. INTEGRITEAM INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2013, Line 9: DEFENDANTS INT TECHNOLOGIES LLC, ITECH US INC., MERIDIAN TECHNOLOGIES INC., PALNAR, RELIANCE GLOBAL SERVICES, INC., S4 CONSULTANTS INC., SPARATAN SOLUTIONS, and MAGANET SOLUTIONS LLC'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint Granted. Defendants shall file their proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. The Court makes no determination as to whether the claims are permissive or compulsory because Defendants would be granted leave under either standard. Pursuant to CCP § 426.50, a Court may only deny leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint if it finds the defendant is acting in bad faith. (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99.) No showing of bad faith has been made here. If the claims are permissive, the Court finds that the substantially similar claims of Campbell & Spangler and Defendants should be tried in the same action. No trial date has yet been set. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARK CATO VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF S.F. (A.P.S.)
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2013, Line 10: DEFENDANTS JULIE PECK, DIMITRIA STATHOPOULOS, TERESA GUILLEN, and CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave one last opportunity to plead facts showing he is excused from complying with the Government Claims Act. (See Shirk v. Vista Unified Sch. Dist. 42 Cal.4th 201, 209 (2007).) Plaintiff has checked box 9B on the form Second Amended Complaint that he is "required to comply with a claims statute and is excused from complying." Plaintiff refers to an "attached declaration." However, the declaration does not set forth facts as to why Plaintiff is excused from complying; it merely cites the Shirk case and the law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PHOENIX LAKE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. GLOBE PROTECT, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Atendance
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 3, 2013, Line 7, PLAINTIFF PHOENIX LAKE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Attendance. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied without prejudice. Defendant is without counsel and cannot represent itself. Plaintiff may re-file this motion within 5 days of the appearance of new counsel or the motion will be moot with the taking of a default judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC VS. KOHL'S CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2013, Line 13: DEFENDANT KOHL'S CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, KOHL'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION, KOHL'S ILLINOIS, INC., A NEVADA CORPORATION'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings All requests for judicial notice are granted. Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. Res judicata applies, as all three prongs are met. (Lyons v. Security Pacific Nat. Bank (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1001, 1005.) First, there was a final judgment on the merits in John Moore v. Benson Mills, Inc., et al. (Santa Clara Superior Court Case No. 112CV237078). "A court-approved settlement acts as a final judgment on the merits for the purposes of res judicata. A judgment entered ... by consent or stipulation, is as conclusive a ... bar as a judgment rendered after trial." (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. ExxonMobil Corp. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 675, 694, citations and quotations omitted.) Second, the party against whom res judicata is raised was in privity with the party to the prior adjudication. Citizens acting in the public interest as private attorney generals are in privity with other citizen enforcers of that same right. (Id. at 689-93.) Third, the issues decided in the Moore action are the same as those presented in this action. The consent judgment in the Moore action explicitly includes, and amends the complaint to include, all placemats, tablecloths, and table pads containing DEHP. (See Declaration of Reuben Yeroushalmi, Ex. H (judicially noticed); Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. 3, paras. 1.6 "Complaint shall be deemed amended to include all Covered Products as defined in this Consent Judgment¿; 2.1.1 defining "Covered Productss.") Additionally, Plaintiff has not established that the narrow public interest exception to res judicata should apply here. (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. ExxonMobil Corp. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 675, 694.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSELITO LAURORA et al VS. RAM K PARMAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Anf Mtn Compel Deft Ram Parmar'S Responses To Discovery, For An Order Establishing Admissions And Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 3, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF JOSELITO LAURORA's Motion To Compel Defendant Ram Parmar's Responses To Discovery, For An Order Establishing Admissions And Requests For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied without prejudice ¿ It appears that Defendant provided some responses. It is not clear whether some particular responses were just skipped because all the responses provided by Defendant were not produced. Moreover, the supplemental responses requested require compliance with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345. As such this motion is denied without prejudice. A corrected and compliant motion can be brought as to these responses within 30 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHI WONG et al VS. BILL WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2013, Line 25: DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG, and CHUNG WAH WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Complaint fails to state a cause of action. (CCP § 430.10(e).) The Complaint is uncertain. (CCP § 430.10(f)).) Inconsistent facts are pled as to the status of the Defendant directors. (Complaint, 13, 18.) Plaintiff Chi Wong lacks standing to sue, as no harm has been alleged to him as an individual. (CCP §367.)
